content: Distance is the total length of the path traveled by a particle. It is a scalar quantity and can never be negative. Displacement is the shortest straight-line distance from the initial to the final position. It is a vector quantity and can be positive, negative, or zero. Magnitude of displacement <= Distance.
Average speed is defined as the total distance traveled divided by the total time taken. v_avg = Total Distance / Total Time. It is a scalar quantity.
Average velocity is defined as the total displacement divided by the total time taken. v_avg_vector = Total Displacement / Total Time. It is a vector quantity.

For motion with constant acceleration 'a', the kinematic equations are: 1) v = u + at, 2) s = ut + 0.5*a*t^2, 3) v^2 = u^2 + 2as. Here u is initial velocity, v is final velocity, s is displacement, and t is time.

For free fall near Earth's surface, acceleration is constant and acts downwards: a = -g (taking upward as positive). The equations become: 1) v = u - gt, 2) h = ut - 0.5*gt^2. At maximum height, velocity v = 0.

Projectile motion is 2D motion under gravity. Horizontal motion has constant velocity (ax = 0). Vertical motion has constant acceleration (ay = -g). The path is a parabola. Time of flight T = 2u*sin(theta)/g. Horizontal Range R = u^2*sin(2*theta)/g.
